title: Rikkar-Du
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Rikkar-du.jpg]] |name=Rikkar-Du |homeworld=[[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]][[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]] |birth= |death=c. [[137 ABY]], Munto Codru |species=[[Codru-Ji]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Green |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners=[[Tassa]] |siblings= |children=[[Jassar]] |affiliation=[[Rikkar-Du's clan]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You know you already have your clan's support, and my father will give you his. If they do agree to unite, you are the only one they will follow.|Tassa, to Rikkar-Du|}} '''Rikkar-Du''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[clan chief]] of the four-[[Limb/Legends|armed]] [[Codru-Ji]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]]. At some point he was kidnapped by a rival [[Tassa's clan|clan]], and eventually [[Marriage/Legends|married]] [[Tassa]], the daughter of that [[Tassa's father|clan's chief]]. Their marriage united their two [[Rikkar-Du's clan|clans]], and the couple [[Reproduction/Legends|conceived]] a son, [[Jassar]]. In approximately [[137 ABY]], Rikkar-Du was approached by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Kruhl]], who proposed that the clan leader ally with [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]], and, in return, the Empire would ensure that he would lead all of Munto Codru's clans. Rikkar-Du refused the offer and instead attempted to [[Codru-Ji clan leader meeting|unite the clans]] against the Empire, but was [[Death/Legends|killed]] [[Assassination on Munto Codru|by Kruhl]] before he could form an alliance. ==Biography== ===Clan leader=== {{Quote|The luckiest day of my life was when your clan kidnapped me. How else would we ever have met? And the union of our two clans with our marriage gave me a vision of all the clans uniting.|Rikkar-Du, to his wife, Tassa|}} [[File:Tassa.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tassa, Rikkar-Du's wife]] [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] and raised on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]], the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Codru-Ji]] Rikkar-Du became the [[Clan chief|chief]] of his [[Rikkar-Du's clan|clan]] during his adult life. At some point he was kidnapped and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] by a rival [[Tassa's clan|clan]], and, during his incarceration, he met his future wife, [[Tassa]], who was the daughter of the [[Tassa's father|clan's chief]]. The two fell in love, and they eventually [[Marriage/Legends|married]], which united their clans and gave Rikkar-Du a high position among the Codru-Ji. During their marriage Rikkar-Du and Tassa had a son, [[Jassar]]. In approximately [[137 ABY]], Rikkar-Du was approached by a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]], [[Darth Kruhl]]. Kruhl had been sent to annex Munto Codru for the Empire, and attempted to negotiate for it peacefully with Rikkar-Du, due to his revered status among his [[Species/Legends|species]]. Kruhl offered to give Rikkar-Du command of all of the Codru-Ji clans if he allowed the Empire to build a base on the planet. Although the uniting of two clans through Rikkar-Du's wedding had given him a vision of all the clans uniting, Rikkar-Du was unwilling to allow the Empire to subjugate his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to achieve it. He resolved to attempt to unite the clans against Kruhl and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Rikkar-Du decided to call a [[Codru-Ji clan leader meeting|meeting]] for all of the clan leaders to discuss a possible defense against the Sith. After revealing his meeting with Kruhl and the Sith's intentions, Rikkar-Du proposed a plan to the clan leaders to let the Empire take the planet, and then abduct their key personnel, using the Codru-Ji's well-known and [[Time/Legends|time]]-honored strategies of staging coups, plotting, and kidnapping. Some of the clan leaders agreed with him, and others pointed out that no matter what they decided, Rikkar-Du would still lead the combined clans. Rikkar-Du stated that he would lead if the clans agreed he should, but he was willing to let another take command as long as they united and opposed the Sith. Nothing was done by the meeting's end, however. Rikkar-Du told the clan leaders there to discuss his plan with the elders in their respective clans, and he called for another meeting the next [[Standard day/Legends|day]]. Afterward, Rikkar-Du met with Tassa and Jassar, though he decided to leave and walk around a [[Temple (Munto Codru)|temple]] after a conversation with his wife. ===Death=== {{Quote|You doomed him as well as yourself with your defiance. Or did you think the Sith would make an enemy and allow him to live?|Darth Kruhl, to Rikkar-Du, speaking of Jassar's death|}} [[File:RikkarSlash.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Rikkar-Du is attacked by Darth Kruhl.]] After hearing of Rikkar-Du's plans to oppose the Sith, Kruhl returned to Munto Codru and met with him in the temple. Knowing that Kruhl was going to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him for his actions against the Sith, Rikkar-Du decided to defend himself using two [[YVH-Series battle droid|security droids]]. Unknown to Rikkar-Du, Kruhl had discovered the Codru-Ji's intention and destroyed the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] before entering the temple. Rikkar-Du was surprised to see Kruhl reveal that he deactivated them by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|levitate]] their severed cranial units and deposit them at the Codru-Ji's [[Limb/Legends|feet]]. Prepared to die, Rikkar-Du drew out four [[Knife/Legends|knives]], one for each arm he possessed, and attacked the Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]]. In defense, Kruhl used his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to [[Assassination on Munto Codru|defeat]] Rikkar-Du by [[Cho mai/Legends|severing]] one of his hands. While Rikkar-Du was dying, Jassar arrived and tried to attack Kruhl to protect his [[Parent/Legends|father]], though he was struck down with ease by Kruhl. After explaining that resistance to the Empire was futile, Kruhl turned his blade on Rikkar-Du and proceeded to kill him. Shortly after Rikkar-Du's death, Kruhl was met by [[Kassek-Ka]], another [[Kassek-Ka's clan|clan]] leader who had attended Rikkar-Du's meeting. Kassek-Ka informed Kruhl that he had killed Tassa and that he was ready to let the Sith take control of Munto Codru. With Rikkar-Du's death, Munto Codru fell to Sith control. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|But you would still command this alliance, Rikkar-Du -- just as the Sith planned.''"
"''If that is the will of the clans. Or pick another.|Kassek-Ka and Rikkar-Du discuss uniting the clans against the Sith|}} Rikkar-Du was a member of the four-armed Codru-Ji species. He had light skin, [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. First and foremost a warrior, Rikkar-Du cared deeply for his planet and was ready to die to stop the Sith's takeover of Munto Codru. He was an adept leader who worked willingly with other clans to unite them, and while he was willing to serve as the leader of the combined clans, he had no compunctions about letting another command as long as they opposed the Sith. Rikkar-Du was devoted to his wife and child, and was devastated when he saw Darth Kruhl kill Jassar, his only son. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|Throughout the galaxy, it is known that no one is better at kidnapping than the Codru Ji! ''No one!''|Rikkar-Du|}} Rikkar-Du was a capable fighter. He fought Darth Kruhl with knives, though quickly lost the fight and was killed. He also believed that he and the other Codru-Ji were some of the best kidnappers in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. ==Equipment== Rikkar-Du utilized four retractable knives in combat, and could wield all simultaneously in battle. He dressed himself in a sleeveless green and gray [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] that had a brown strap along with a sleeveless yellow undershirt, a brown cloth [[Belt/Legends|belt]], and brown [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. He wore a beige bandanna, a headdress with white and red beads, as well as a [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]] and [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelets]] on each of his four arms all with the same color beads as the headdress. Additionally, both of his ears were pierced with three [[Piercing/Legends|piercings]]. ==Behind the scenes== Rikkar-Du appeared in the [[Legacy (2006) 13|thirteenth issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'', which was written by [[John Ostrander]], penciled by [[Colin Wilson (artist)|Colin Wilson]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] on [[June 13]], [[2007]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54385/star_wars_legacy_2006_13|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) #13}} Although the comic never specified whether the letter ''D'' in Rikkar-Du's name is capitalized, it was capitalized in his entry in the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.{{CSWECite|III|100|Rikkar-Du}} ==Appearances== *[[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Rikkar-Du}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Rikkar-du |es=Rikkar-du }} [[Category:Chiefs]] [[Category:Codru-Ji]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
